msgs.text:
Turn 56 — standalone descent note and final topological audit I re-read only the three current attachments: mega_prompt(10852).md, problem_statement(10155).md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso(55).md.

The decision remains:ΓS≅GΓH.More precisely, the sweep now supports the stronger statement that there is a continuous automorphism Φ:F→F over G such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup, this descends to an over-G isomorphism

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.

That is,ΓS≅GΓH.1. Fixed orientation The source relator orientation must be fixed once and for all:ρS:=rS−1.This is the orientation whose abelianized row was used throughout the sweep. It is harmless because⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F=⟨⟨rS−1⟩⟩F.

So the actual construction targetsΦ(ρS)=rH.2. The H1

Nielsen start is invertibleLetΛ=Z2 [[G]].

With the fixed orientation ρS=rS−1, the abelianized relation rows arewS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E),where E=e⟨τ⟩.The row comparison splits by E. On the (1−E)-piece the rows are (σ,−2),(−2,σ),so they are related by swapping the two generators.

On the E-piece, writes=σ2,a=σ 1−π2,and then split by the idempotent f=e⟨a⟩. On the dangerous Ef-piece the rows become (s+3,−2),(s2+1,s−1).

The correct row-action matrix isU0=( s−1s−212).

Indeed,(s+3,−2)U0=(s2+1,s−1),and det(U0)=2(s−1)−(s−2)=s,which is a unit.The E(1−f)-piece is handled by the unit matrix from the earlier sweep. Therefore the pieces glue to a global

U∈GL2(Λ)carrying wS to wH.This fixes the first Magnus layer of the desired filtered Nielsen transformation.

3. Non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled

After applying the H1 -change U, every non-Ef correction equation has a unit coefficient. Thus all higher errors on those pieces are killed by ordinary filtered IA corrections.So the only obstruction tower is the local Ef-piece.

4. The residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower vanishes On the residual Ef-piece, one works in the completed mod-2 Magnus algebra with

X=1+A,Y=1+B,and residual equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q=X2.

Let L∙⊂F2⟨⟨A,B⟩⟩ be the free restricted Lie algebra of primitives. If the first nonzero error has degree n, it lies in Ln. The correction boundary is∂n−1:Ln−1⊕Ln−1→Ln,(U,V)↦[A,U]+[B,V].

The restricted-Lie recursion isLn/im∂n−1≅{0,Ln/2[2],n odd,n even.The finite sweep established the base block, including the difficult residual degree 16 lower-base toggle, and then Turn 18 proved the uniform inductionCm=Lm (m≥5).Thus every odd layer is an ordinary boundary, and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression

W↦W[2].Hence the residual obstruction tower vanishes to all orders.

5. The Ef-coefficient tower also vanishes

Locally on the dangerous coefficient piece,

R=EfΛ≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1.

Before normalization the local row is(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t).

But this row is exactly Nielsen-equivalent over R to (2,t).

Indeed,(s2+1)−(s+1)(s−1)=2,so right multiplication byN=(1−(s+1)01)sends(s2+1,s−1)to (2,t).

Thus the apparent 2t+t2 term is a coordinate artifact.Nowgr(2,t)

R≃F2 [α,β],α=in(2),β=in(t).

The normalized row is the regular Koszul row (α,β).

The first t-layer R/(2,t2) is contractible. The first 2-Bockstein layer is controlled byHd(2)=Ld[2]/imθd0,whereθd0:ker(Ld⊕Ld[A,−]+[B,−]Ld+1)→Ld[2],(u,v)↦u[2]+v[2].

The sweep establishedBd↠Hd(2)(d≥4).The base range 4≤d≤8 is covered by the nonlinear certificates D.1–D.4, and the range d≥9 follows by the coefficient-safe induction.For higher (2,t)-adic layers, one uses the normalized Koszul–Witt cascade. Actual pro-2 representatives do not produce only a single Frobenius square term; they produce a Witt cascade. Ordered by Magnus root degree first, every forced tail lands later in the induction. Koszul syzygies slide β-powers to pure α-powers, pure α-multiples are killed by the already-established first 2-Bockstein maps Bd↠Hd(2), and coefficient-free terms fall back into the closed residual tower.Therefore every finite Ef-local coefficient obstruction is killable.

6. Certificate ledger is now closed inside the sweep

The nonlinear certificate blocks are:

BlockD.1D.2D.3D.4

Content ker∂8↠L5[2]residual degree-16 λ-toggleA2=R/(4,t),d=4,μ4 -toggleθ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2]

Status explicit Bockstein witnesses explicit lower-base witness rank jump 60→61explicit quotient witness The last remaining executable certificate was D.3. Turn 54 records the verifier output rankD5,6,7=60,and rank(D5,6,7μ4B4)=61.

Since H4(2)is one-dimensional, this proves that the continuation kernel toggles the fixed μ4=1 scalar to μ4=0.Thus the finite coefficient nonemptiness proof is supported by the recorded certificates.

7. Finite filtered Nielsen compactnessLet

M=Λ⟨⟨X0,X1⟩⟩,I=(X0,X1).

For a degree cutoff N and an open two-sided ideal J⊂Λ, definePN,J:=P∩(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is a finite 2-group, and the system is separated:N,J⋂PN,J=1.

Define SN,J to be the finite set of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data(y0,y1,cσ,cτ)∈(P/PN,J)4 such that:the first Magnus layer of y0,y1 is UmodJ;cσ,cτ satisfy the finite cocycle relation encoding τσ=τ2;the induced twisted substitution sendsρS=rS−1 to rH modulo PN,J;4. the substitution is filtered.Each datum gives an actual finite-kernel over-G automorphism: its first layer is invertible, so the finite Frattini criterion applies to P/PN,J. This avoids any appeal to Hopficity of the full infinite-rank pro-2 group P.The residual induction, coefficient Koszul–Witt induction, and certificates D.1–D.4 proveSN,J=∅ for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation.

The sets SN,J are functorial under refinement. Since they are finite and nonempty, compactness givesN,JlimSN,J=∅.Choose a compatible inverse-limit system. It gives a continuous filtered Nielsen automorphism α:P→P and a continuous cocycle c:G→P.

Together they define an over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G→F by the usual twisted formula Φ(pg)=α(p)c(g)g.

At every finite level,Φ(ρS)≡rH (modPN,J).

Intersecting over all N,J givesΦ(rS−1)=rH inside F.

Therefore Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.

Passing to quotients givesΓS=F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F=ΓH.

Because Φ is over G, the descended isomorphism satisfiesπH∘Φ=πS.

HenceΓS≅GΓH.

Structural audit The final argument is no longer driven by a single exceptional case. The exceptional-looking layers all fed into uniform mechanisms:

The residual tower collapses because Cm=Lm for all m≥5. The local coefficient tower collapses because the row normalizes exactly to (2,t), after which Koszul–Witt induction reduces higher coefficient ghosts to the first 2-Bockstein layer. The d=4 coefficient issue is resolved by the same principle as residual degree 16: a fixed gauge shows a false obstruction, but the full continuation torsor moves it.The only remaining caveat is external reproducibility: the current attachments record verifier outputs and rank certificates, but they do not include the actual verifier scripts or full matrices. That is an appendix/presentation issue, not a mathematical obstruction inside the sweep ledger.

ESTABLISHED: The sweep proves ΓS≅GΓH. More precisely, there is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, so Φ descends to an isomorphism ΓS→ΓH commuting with the canonical projections to G.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains within the attached sweep. The remaining gap is presentation-level reproducibility outside the transcript: a standalone appendix should include the verifier scripts or explicit matrices supporting the recorded rank certificates.

NEXT: Write the polished standalone proof as a compact note with an appendix: H1 Nielsen matrix, residual restricted-Lie induction, coefficient Koszul–Witt induction, certificates D.1–D.4 including the Turn 54 stack-rank 60→61, and filtered Nielsen compactness descent.[TURN56-STANDALONE-DESCENT-NOTE-ISO]
